_note: GAP.7 the cold-open prologue shown once at the start of a new game. Composed by engine/intro.py from the hero's class + world + first goal.

openings.warrior: Steel has been your tongue since boyhood, and the shield-wall your first home.
openings.wizard: You have traded sleep for grimoires, and count power in syllables few dare speak.
openings.rogue: The world showed you its locked doors early. You simply learned to open them.
openings.ranger: The roads end where you begin. The wild has always kept your counsel.
openings.cleric: A vow set you on this path — and the vow, in turn, has taken root in you.
openings.bard: You have sung for supper in a hundred tap-rooms, and heard every rumour twice.
openings.merchant: You learned the weight of a coin before the weight of a blade — and both have their uses.
openings.default: You came up hard, made your own way, and the road has never once let you rest.

worlds.default: Now Oakvale opens before you — a green country of village hearths, deep forest, and older, darker places that the maps mark only with warnings.
worlds.oakvale: Now the great town of Oakvale opens before you — its walls and wards, its crowded wards and, beneath the streets, a stair going down into the dark.
worlds.castle: Now the Bloodstone realm opens before you — a curtain-walled fortress, a king's court, and a fault-line of intrigue running straight through the throne room.
worlds.realistic: Now a whole living world opens before you — mountains and coasts shaped by a thousand years of history, its ruins still remembering the wars that raised them.

call: What you make of it is yours alone to write. The first page is blank.
hints: Press M for the map, Y for your journal, C for your character.
An Adventurers' Board hangs in the tavern (E) — your first tasks wait there.
Talk to everyone (T). The world remembers what you do.
